              "Agents sourced from roborev_agent_performance in unified.duckdb (no repo column â€” no confidential filtering needed). ",
              "Cost per review is shown only when total_cost_usd is available; NA values display as â€”. ",
              "Data refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/ for CI serving.")
          )
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(
            card_header("Review Backlog Age (#146 Q15)"),
            tags$table(class = "table table-sm table-dark mb-2",
              tags$thead(tags$tr(tags$th("Metric"), tags$th("Value"))),
              tags$tbody(
                tags$tr(tags$td("Open reviews"),   tags$td(textOutput("rv_ba_n_open",   inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("Median age"),      tags$td(textOutput("rv_ba_median",   inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("90th pct age"),    tags$td(textOutput("rv_ba_p90",      inline = TRUE))),
                tags$tr(tags$td("Oldest review"),   tags$td(textOutput("rv_ba_max",      inline = TRUE)))
              )
            ),
            uiOutput("rv_ba_chart"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
              "Horizontal bar chart (Cleveland-style): number of open reviews per age bucket ",
              "(0-1d / 1-3d / 3-7d / 7-14d / >14d). ",
              "Age = now minus enqueued_at from ~/.roborev/reviews.db. ",
              "Confidential repos (mycare, crypto, solwatch, swarms) excluded. ",
              "Source: roborev_backlog_age.json â€” refreshed on each local export run and committed to inst/extdata/.")
          )
        )
      )
    )
  ),

  nav_panel("Reference", value = "Reference",
    navset_tab(
      nav_panel("Calibration", value = "Calibration",
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(6, 6),
          card(card_header("Reliability Diagram"), uiOutput("cal_reliability"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Predicted confidence vs actual accuracy by bucket. Diagonal = perfect calibration.")),
          card(card_header("Brier Score Trend"), uiOutput("cal_brier"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Rolling Brier score over time (0 = perfect, 0.25 = uninformative)"))
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(6, 6),
          card(card_header("Accuracy by Task Type"), uiOutput("cal_by_type"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Success rate per task category")),
          card(card_header("Accuracy by Project"), uiOutput("cal_by_project"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "Success rate per project Â· sparse data expected (only resolved predictions logged)"))
        ),
        layout_columns(col_widths = c(12),
          card(card_header("Prediction Log"), DTOutput("cal_log"),
            card_footer(class = "text-muted small", "All predictions with stated confidence, outcome, and notes"))
        )
      ),
      nav_panel("Glossary", value = "Glossary", icon = icon("book"),
        # Architecture card â€” issue #176 increment 1
        # Approach: Mermaid flowchart TB (top-to-bottom) with clickable nodes (securityLevel loose)
        # + definition list with Tippy hover popups (body-size, >=2 sentences, >=1 link)
        # DL fallback chosen over Tippy-on-SVG-node because SVG node IDs are unstable
        # in WebAssembly/Shinylive context (Mermaid ID generation timing).
        card(card_header("Architecture â€” Data & Build Flow"),
          tags$div(class = "p-3 arch-mermaid-wrap",
            # Mermaid diagram: refresh-cron -> committed-data -> CI-render -> dashboard/email
            tags$div(class = "mermaid", "
flowchart TB
    classDef default fill:#999999,stroke:#CC0000,stroke-width:1px,color:#000000;
    Logs[\"Claude / Codex / Gemini<br/>usage logs (~/.claude)\"]
    Cron[\"Refresh cron â€”<br/>refresh_and_preserve.sh<br/>(every 12h)\"]
    Tools[\"ccusage CLI Â· Gemini API Â· Codex\"]
    Data[\"Committed data â€”<br/>inst/extdata/*.json, *.duckdb\"]
    CI[\"CI render â€”<br/>deploy-dashboard.yaml\"]
    Dash[\"Shinylive dashboard<br/>(this page)\"]
    Email[\"Daily email â€”<br/>send_daily_email.R<br/>(08:00 UTC)\"]
    Logs --> Cron
    Cron --> Tools
    Tools --> Data
    Data --> CI
    CI --> Dash
    Data --> Email
    click Cron \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh\" \"View refresh script\" _blank
    click Data \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata\" \"Browse committed data\" _blank
    click CI \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/.github/workflows/deploy-dashboard.yaml\" \"View CI workflow\" _blank
    click Dash \"https://johngavin.github.io/llmtelemetry/\" \"Open live dashboard\" _blank
    click Email \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/inst/scripts/send_daily_email.R\" \"View email script\" _blank
            "),
            tags$p(class = "text-muted small mt-2",
              "Click highlighted nodes to open the relevant source or dataset. ",
              "Hover over component names below for descriptions."),
            # Definition list with Tippy hover popups â€” hover-popup-standard rule compliance:
            # each term has >=2 sentences, >=1 embedded link, font-size 1rem (via arch theme CSS)
            tags$dl(class = "arch-dl mt-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.95em;",
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Claude / Codex / Gemini usage logs</b> â€” Raw telemetry written by Claude Code, ",
                  "OpenAI Codex, and Gemini CLI as JSON/text to <code>~/.claude/</code> and related dirs. ",
                  "These are the primary input to the entire pipeline â€” without them no cost or session ",
                  "data can be derived. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/ryoppippi/ccusage' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>ccusage</a> ",
                  "for how they are read."
                ),
                "Usage Logs"
              ),
              tags$dd("Raw JSON / text telemetry from Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI (~/.claude). Primary pipeline input."),

              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Refresh cron â€” exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh</b> â€” A shell script scheduled every 12 hours ",
                  "that calls ccusage, the Gemini API, and Codex exporters, then commits the resulting JSON and ",
                  "DuckDB files to the repository. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>refresh_and_preserve.sh on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "Refresh Cron"
              ),
              tags$dd("Shell script (every 12h) that runs data exporters and commits the results."),

              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>ccusage CLI Â· Gemini API Â· Codex</b> â€” The three data-extraction tools: ccusage reads ",
                  "Claude Code session logs, the Gemini API returns token usage, and Codex export parses ",
                  "OpenAI usage. Each produces structured JSON that the cron commits. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/ryoppippi/ccusage' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>ccusage on GitHub</a> ",
                  "for Claude Code extraction details."
                ),
                "Extraction Tools"
              ),
              tags$dd("ccusage CLI (Claude), Gemini API, and Codex exporter â€” produce the committed JSON data."),

              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Committed data â€” inst/extdata/*.json, *.duckdb</b> â€” The authoritative data store: JSON ",
                  "files (daily, sessions, blocks, calibration, git-recon) and DuckDB files committed to the ",
                  "repository by the refresh cron. These files are the single source of truth for all dashboard ",
                  "tabs. ",
                  "Browse at <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>inst/extdata on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "Committed Data"
              ),
              tags$dd("JSON + DuckDB files committed to inst/extdata/ â€” the dashboard's single source of truth."),

              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>CI render â€” deploy-dashboard.yaml</b> â€” A GitHub Actions workflow that runs on every push, ",
                  "renders this Quarto Shinylive document, and deploys it to GitHub Pages. The workflow also ",
                  "validates the Mermaid syntax and runs R CMD check. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/.github/workflows/deploy-dashboard.yaml' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>deploy-dashboard.yaml on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "CI Render"
              ),
              tags$dd("GitHub Actions workflow: renders and deploys the dashboard to GitHub Pages on every push."),

              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Shinylive dashboard (this page)</b> â€” A Shiny app compiled to WebAssembly and served as a ",
                  "static page via GitHub Pages â€” no R server required. All data is fetched from the committed ",
                  "JSON files at load time. ",
                  "Live at <a href='https://johngavin.github.io/llmtelemetry/' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>",
                  "johngavin.github.io/llmtelemetry</a>."
                ),
                "Shinylive Dashboard"
              ),
              tags$dd("This page â€” a static Shinylive/WebAssembly app served from GitHub Pages. No server required."),

              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Daily email â€” send_daily_email.R (08:00 UTC)</b> â€” An R script scheduled at 08:00 UTC that ",
                  "reads the committed data, computes a daily summary (cost, tokens, commits), and sends it as an ",
                  "HTML email. Runs independently of the dashboard render. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/inst/scripts/send_daily_email.R' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>send_daily_email.R on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "Daily Email"
              ),
              tags$dd("R script (08:00 UTC) â€” reads committed data and sends an HTML daily summary email.")
            )
          ),
          card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
            "Architecture of the llmtelemetry refresh pipeline. ",
            "Click nodes in the diagram to open the relevant GitHub source or dataset.")
        ),
        # Workflow card â€” issue #176 increment 2
        # Shows the end-to-end refresh lifecycle: cron triggers -> data pipeline -> CI -> publish
        card(card_header("Workflow â€” Refresh Lifecycle"),
          tags$div(class = "p-3 arch-mermaid-wrap",
            tags$div(class = "mermaid", "
flowchart TB
    classDef default fill:#999999,stroke:#CC0000,stroke-width:1px,color:#000000;
    Cron12h[\"Cron (every 12h) â€”<br/>refresh_and_preserve.sh\"]
    FetchCC[\"ccusage fetch â€”<br/>Claude session logs\"]
    FetchGem[\"Gemini API fetch â€”<br/>token usage\"]
    FetchCodex[\"Codex export â€”<br/>OpenAI usage\"]
    Sanitize[\"Sanitize & redact (privacy)\"]
    Commit[\"git commit + push â€”<br/>inst/extdata\"]
    CI[\"GitHub Actions â€”<br/>deploy-dashboard.yaml\"]
    Pages[\"GitHub Pages â€”<br/>static Shinylive site\"]
    Email[\"send_daily_email.R<br/>(08:00 UTC)\"]
    Cron12h --> FetchCC
    Cron12h --> FetchGem
    Cron12h --> FetchCodex
    FetchCC --> Sanitize
    FetchGem --> Sanitize
    FetchCodex --> Sanitize
    Sanitize --> Commit
    Commit --> CI
    CI --> Pages
    Commit --> Email
    click Cron12h \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh\" \"View refresh script\" _blank
    click CI \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/.github/workflows/deploy-dashboard.yaml\" \"View CI workflow\" _blank
    click Pages \"https://johngavin.github.io/llmtelemetry/\" \"Open live dashboard\" _blank
    click Email \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/inst/scripts/send_daily_email.R\" \"View email script\" _blank
            "),
            tags$p(class = "text-muted small mt-2",
              "Click highlighted nodes to open the relevant source or workflow. ",
              "Hover over component names below for descriptions."),
            tags$dl(class = "arch-dl mt-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.95em;",
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Cron (every 12h) â€” refresh_and_preserve.sh</b> â€” A shell script triggered ",
                  "every 12 hours that orchestrates all data fetching, sanitization, and committing steps. ",
                  "It preserves existing data before overwriting, ensuring no telemetry is lost. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>refresh_and_preserve.sh on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "12h Refresh Cron"
              ),
              tags$dd("Shell script run every 12 hours â€” orchestrates fetch, sanitize, commit, and push."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>ccusage fetch</b> â€” Calls the ccusage CLI to read Claude Code session JSON logs ",
                  "from <code>~/.claude/</code> and export them as structured JSON (daily, sessions, blocks). ",
                  "ccusage parses raw Claude Code telemetry into cost and token summaries. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/ryoppippi/ccusage' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>",
                  "ccusage on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "ccusage Fetch"
              ),
              tags$dd("Reads Claude Code logs from ~/.claude and exports daily/sessions/blocks JSON."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Gemini API fetch</b> â€” Queries the Google Gemini API to retrieve token usage statistics ",
                  "for Gemini CLI sessions. Results are stored in <code>gemini_usage.duckdb</code>. ",
                  "See <a href='https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>",
                  "Gemini API docs</a>."
                ),
                "Gemini API Fetch"
              ),
              tags$dd("Queries Google Gemini API for token usage; stored in gemini_usage.duckdb."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>Sanitize & redact</b> â€” Removes personally identifiable information and confidential ",
                  "project paths from the telemetry before committing to the public repository. ",
                  "This step ensures GDPR and privacy compliance for the published data. ",
                  "See the <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>sanitize logic in refresh_and_preserve.sh</a>."
                ),
                "Sanitize & Redact"
              ),
              tags$dd("Removes PII and confidential paths before committing to the public repo."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>GitHub Actions â€” deploy-dashboard.yaml</b> â€” Triggered on every push to main, ",
                  "this workflow renders the Quarto Shinylive document and deploys it to GitHub Pages. ",
                  "It also runs R CMD check and validates the output. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/.github/workflows/deploy-dashboard.yaml' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>deploy-dashboard.yaml on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "CI Render & Deploy"
              ),
              tags$dd("GitHub Actions renders Quarto Shinylive and deploys to GitHub Pages on every push."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>send_daily_email.R (08:00 UTC)</b> â€” An R script scheduled at 08:00 UTC that reads ",
                  "the latest committed data, computes a daily cost/token/commit summary, and sends it as ",
                  "an HTML email to the configured recipients. Runs independently of the dashboard CI. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/inst/scripts/send_daily_email.R' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>send_daily_email.R on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "Daily Email (08:00 UTC)"
              ),
              tags$dd("R script (08:00 UTC) reads committed data and sends an HTML daily summary email.")
            )
          ),
          card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
            "End-to-end refresh lifecycle for llmtelemetry. ",
            "Click nodes in the diagram to open the relevant source or workflow.")
        ),
        # Datasets card â€” issue #176 increment 2
        # Shows the committed data files in inst/extdata and what each holds
        card(card_header("Datasets â€” Committed Data Files"),
          tags$div(class = "p-3 arch-mermaid-wrap",
            tags$div(class = "mermaid", "
flowchart LR
    classDef default fill:#999999,stroke:#CC0000,stroke-width:1px,color:#000000;
    ExtData[\"inst/extdata/ â€”<br/>committed data root\"]
    CCDaily[\"ccusage_daily_all.json â€”<br/>daily cost & tokens\"]
    CCSess[\"ccusage_sessions_all.json â€”<br/>per-session records\"]
    GemDB[\"gemini_usage.duckdb â€”<br/>Gemini token history\"]
    UsageDB[\"llm_usage_history.duckdb â€”<br/>unified usage store\"]
    Roborev[\"roborev_summary.json â€”<br/>code review metrics\"]
    Preds[\"predictions.json â€”<br/>calibration log\"]
    Cmonitor[\"cmonitor_daily.txt â€”<br/>system cost monitor\"]
    ExtData --> CCDaily
    ExtData --> CCSess
    ExtData --> GemDB
    ExtData --> UsageDB
    ExtData --> Roborev
    ExtData --> Preds
    ExtData --> Cmonitor
    click ExtData \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata\" \"Browse all data files\" _blank
    click CCDaily \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata\" \"Browse extdata\" _blank
    click GemDB \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata\" \"Browse extdata\" _blank
    click UsageDB \"https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata\" \"Browse extdata\" _blank
            "),
            tags$p(class = "text-muted small mt-2",
              "Click highlighted nodes to browse the data files on GitHub. ",
              "Hover over file names below for descriptions."),
            tags$dl(class = "arch-dl mt-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.95em;",
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>ccusage_daily_all.json</b> â€” Daily aggregated cost and token totals exported by ccusage. ",
                  "Each record covers one calendar day and includes input, output, cache-creation, and ",
                  "cache-read tokens plus total cost in USD. Powers the Cost and Tokens dashboard tabs. ",
                  "Browse at <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>inst/extdata on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "ccusage_daily_all.json"
              ),
              tags$dd("Daily cost and token aggregates from ccusage. Powers Cost and Tokens tabs."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>ccusage_sessions_all.json</b> â€” Per-API-conversation records from ccusage. Each record ",
                  "represents a single Claude Code conversation with start time, duration, token counts, ",
                  "project, and model. Powers the Sessions dashboard tab. ",
                  "Browse at <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/tree/main/inst/extdata' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>inst/extdata on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "ccusage_sessions_all.json"
              ),
              tags$dd("Per-API-conversation records: start, duration, tokens, model, project."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>gemini_usage.duckdb</b> â€” DuckDB database holding Gemini CLI token usage history. ",
                  "Queried by duckplyr in the dashboard to show Gemini cost alongside Claude costs. ",
                  "Updated every 12 hours by the refresh cron. ",
                  "See <a href='https://duckdb.org/' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>DuckDB</a> for the format."
                ),
                "gemini_usage.duckdb"
              ),
              tags$dd("DuckDB file: Gemini CLI token usage history, updated every 12 hours."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>llm_usage_history.duckdb</b> â€” Unified DuckDB store combining Claude, Gemini, and Codex ",
                  "usage records into a single queryable database. Supports cross-provider cost comparison ",
                  "in the dashboard. ",
                  "See <a href='https://duckdb.org/' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>DuckDB</a> for the format."
                ),
                "llm_usage_history.duckdb"
              ),
              tags$dd("Unified DuckDB: all providers (Claude, Gemini, Codex) in one queryable store."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>roborev_summary.json</b> â€” JSON export of code-review metrics from the roborev tool. ",
                  "Includes review counts, open/closed findings, and per-project severity breakdown. ",
                  "Powers the Git/Commits dashboard tab. ",
                  "See <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry' target='_blank' rel='noopener'>",
                  "llmtelemetry on GitHub</a>."
                ),
                "roborev_summary.json"
              ),
              tags$dd("Code-review metrics from roborev: counts, findings, severity by project."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>predictions.json</b> â€” The calibration log: each record is a stated prediction with ",
                  "confidence, resolution date, actual outcome, and notes. Used to compute Brier score and ",
                  "the reliability diagram in the Calibration tab. ",
                  "See <a href='https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Calibration_(statistics)' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>statistical calibration on Wikipedia</a>."
                ),
                "predictions.json"
              ),
              tags$dd("Calibration log: predictions with confidence, outcome, and notes for Brier score."),
              tags$dt(
                `data-arch-tip` = paste0(
                  "<b>cmonitor_daily.txt</b> â€” Daily system-level cost-monitor output (plain text). Captures ",
                  "total API spend and burn rate at a system level, independent of ccusage. Provides a ",
                  "cross-check against the ccusage daily totals. ",
                  "See the <a href='https://github.com/JohnGavin/llmtelemetry/blob/main/exec/refresh_and_preserve.sh' ",
                  "target='_blank' rel='noopener'>refresh script</a> for how it is generated."
                ),
                "cmonitor_daily.txt"
              ),
              tags$dd("System-level daily cost monitor output â€” cross-checks ccusage totals.")
            )
          ),
          card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
            "All data files committed to inst/extdata/ on every 12-hour refresh. ",
            "Click nodes to browse on GitHub.")
        ),
        card(card_header("Metric Definitions"),
          tags$div(class = "p-3", style = "color: #e0e0e0; font-size: 0.9em;",
            navset_tab(
              nav_panel("Cost",
                tags$dl(class = "mt-2",
                  tags$dt("Total Cost ($)"), tags$dd("Sum of API charges for input, output, and cache tokens. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models/overview#api-models", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Anthropic pricing]")),
                  tags$dt("Cost per MTok"), tags$dd("Cost per million tokens â€” efficiency metric comparing model tiers."),
                  tags$dt("Cumulative Cost"), tags$dd("Running total of spend over time. Useful for burn-rate forecasting."),
                  tags$dt("Est. Cost by Project"), tags$dd("âš  Approximate: daily cost Ă— (project session time / total session time). No per-project billing from API.")
                )
              ),
              nav_panel("Tokens",
                tags$dl(class = "mt-2",
                  tags$dt("Input Tokens"), tags$dd("Tokens sent to the model (prompts, context)."),
                  tags$dt("Output Tokens"), tags$dd("Tokens generated by the model (responses)."),
                  tags$dt("Cache Creation"), tags$dd("Tokens written to prompt cache (first use). Billed at 25% of base input rate. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Anthropic caching docs]")),
                  tags$dt("Cache Read"), tags$dd("Tokens retrieved from cache (subsequent uses). Billed at 10% of base input rate â€” significant cost saving for repeated context. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Anthropic caching docs]"))
                )
              ),
              nav_panel("Sessions",
                tags$dl(class = "mt-2",
                  tags$dt("API Conversation"), tags$dd("Single Claude Code conversation. Short durations (0-5 min) are normal â€” each context compaction or restart creates a new conversation. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Claude Code docs]")),
                  tags$dt("Duration (min)"), tags$dd("Time from conversation start to end. NOT user work time â€” excludes idle periods between messages."),
                  tags$dt("Block"), tags$dd("Contiguous coding period from ccusage; gaps >30 min create new blocks. More meaningful than API conversations as a work-session metric. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://github.com/ryoppippi/ccusage", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[ccusage]"))
                )
              ),
              nav_panel("Git",
                tags$dl(class = "mt-2",
                  tags$dt("Bus Factor"), tags$dd("Risk metric: if 1 person has >60% of commits, project is at risk if they leave. Low bus factor = knowledge concentration. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bus_factor", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Wikipedia]")),
                  tags$dt("Velocity Trend"), tags$dd("Commits per month â€” indicates project momentum. Declining velocity may signal reduced activity or scope completion."),
                  tags$dt("Commit Timing"), tags$dd("Heatmap of when commits happen by hour and day. Shows work patterns and potential burnout signals."),
                  tags$dt("File Growth"), tags$dd("6-month rolling aggregate of files added vs deleted. Net positive = codebase growing."),
                  tags$dt("Churn Hotspots"), tags$dd("Files modified most often. High churn = refactoring candidates or unstable design. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Software_entropy", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Software entropy]")),
                  tags$dt("Bug Hotspots"), tags$dd("Files frequently touched by fix/bug commits. May indicate code quality issues."),
                  tags$dt("TODO/FIXME Debt"), tags$dd("Count of TODO, FIXME, HACK markers. Technical debt that should be tracked and resolved. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Technical_debt", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Technical debt]")),
                  tags$dt("Crisis Commits"), tags$dd("Reverts, hotfixes, emergency commits. High count = unstable releases or reactive development culture.")
                )
              ),
              nav_panel("Calibration",
                tags$dl(class = "mt-2",
                  tags$dt("Calibration"), tags$dd("How well predicted confidence matches actual outcomes. 80% confident predictions should succeed ~80% of the time. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Calibration_(statistics)", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Wikipedia]")),
                  tags$dt("Brier Score"), tags$dd("Mean squared error of probabilistic predictions. 0 = perfect, 0.25 = no better than guessing 50%. Lower is better. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brier_score", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[Wikipedia]")),
                  tags$dt("Reliability Diagram"), tags$dd("Plot of predicted confidence vs actual success rate. Points on diagonal = perfectly calibrated. Above = overconfident. Below = underconfident."),
                  tags$dt("Overconfidence"), tags$dd("Predictions above the reliability diagram diagonal â€” stated confidence exceeds actual accuracy."),
                  tags$dt("Underconfidence"), tags$dd("Predictions below the diagonal â€” actual accuracy exceeds stated confidence (rare but possible).")
                )
              ),
              nav_panel("Tools",
                tags$dl(class = "mt-2",
                  tags$dt("Shinylive"), tags$dd("R Shiny apps running entirely in the browser via WebAssembly â€” no server required. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://posit-dev.github.io/r-shinylive/", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[posit-dev.github.io]")),
                  tags$dt("WebR / WebAssembly"), tags$dd("R compiled to WebAssembly, running natively in the browser. Powers the Shinylive embedding in this dashboard. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://docs.r-wasm.org/webr/latest/", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[docs.r-wasm.org]")),
                  tags$dt("DuckDB-WASM"), tags$dd("DuckDB compiled to WebAssembly â€” SQL analytics in the browser, no server. Used here for Parquet queries. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://duckdb.org/docs/clients/wasm/overview.html", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[DuckDB WASM docs]")),
                  tags$dt("duckplyr"), tags$dd("Tidyverse-syntax DuckDB queries in R â€” replaces raw SQL strings. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://duckplyr.tidyverse.org/", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[duckplyr.tidyverse.org]")),
                  tags$dt("ECharts"), tags$dd("Apache ECharts â€” the JavaScript charting library powering all interactive charts. ",
                    tags$a(href = "https://echarts.apache.org/en/index.html", target = "_blank", rel = "noopener noreferrer", style = "color:#7cb5ec;", "[echarts.apache.org]"))
                )
              )
            )
          ),
          card_footer(class = "text-muted small",
            "Reference definitions for interpreting dashboard metrics. Links open in a new tab. Hover over charts for tooltips.")
        )
      )
    )
  ),

  footer = tags$footer(
    class = "text-muted small text-center py-2",
    style = "background-color: #1a1a1a;",
    tags$a(href = "https://github.com/johngavin/llmtelemetry", style = "color: #7cb5ec;", "GitHub"),
    " | Data: ccusage, Gemini, Codex, cmonitor, unified.duckdb, git-recon, predictions",
    " | Built: ", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")
  ),
  id = "tabs"
)

# --- Server -------------------------------------------------------------------
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  cutoff <- reactive(date_max - input$horizon)

  cc_d <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(cc_daily)) return(data.frame())
    cc_daily[cc_daily$date >= cutoff(), ]
  })
  gem_d <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(gem_daily)) return(data.frame())
    gem_daily[gem_daily$date >= cutoff(), ]
  })
  codex_d <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(codex_daily)) return(data.frame())
    codex_daily[codex_daily$date >= cutoff(), ]
  })
  # codex_d_proj: when specific projects are selected, filter by canonical_project.
  # When "All projects" is selected (resolve_sel returns NULL), returns codex_d() unchanged.
  codex_d_proj <- reactive({
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    d   <- codex_d()
    if (is.null(sel) || nrow(d) == 0) return(d)
    if (!"canonical_project" %in% names(d)) return(d)
    d[d$canonical_project %in% sel, , drop = FALSE]
  })
  # cc_d_proj: when specific projects are selected, weight tokens from tok_proj
  # (tokens_by_project.json). When "All projects" is selected, resolve_sel()
  # returns NULL so is.null(sel) is TRUE, and we fall back to the authoritative
  # cc_d() (ccusage_daily.json) â€” no dates or live projects dropped.
  # When "None" is selected, resolve_sel() returns character(0) and
  # d[d$project %in% sel, ] returns 0 rows (correct empty result).
  cc_d_proj <- reactive({
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    if (is.null(sel) || !has_rows(tok_proj)) return(cc_d())
    d <- tok_proj
    if (!is.null(cutoff())) d <- d[as.Date(d$date) >= cutoff(), ]
    d <- d[d$project %in% sel, , drop = FALSE]
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(data.frame())
    # Rename to match cc_daily column names so token charts need no changes
    names(d)[names(d) == "est_total_tokens"]   <- "totalTokens"
    names(d)[names(d) == "est_input_tokens"]   <- "inputTokens"
    names(d)[names(d) == "est_output_tokens"]  <- "outputTokens"
    names(d)[names(d) == "est_cache_creation"] <- "cacheCreation"
    names(d)[names(d) == "est_cache_read"]     <- "cacheRead"
    # Aggregate across selected projects -> one row per date
    aggregate(
      cbind(totalTokens, inputTokens, outputTokens, cacheCreation, cacheRead) ~ date,
      data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE
    )
  })

  # --- Phase B: one-shot sidebar population from projects_master.json ----------
  # CRITICAL: choices are populated ONCE on session start by virtue of being
  # called directly in the server body (no reactive wrapper). The original
  # observe(..., once = TRUE) was invalid â€” `once` is an observeEvent() arg,
  # not an observe() arg, and broke the entire server function.
  # This fixes W4 (stickiness: selector resetting on time-horizon change).
  local({
    master_choices <- c(
      "All projects" = "__ALL__",
      "None"         = "__NONE__",
      setNames(all_projects, all_projects)
    )
    updateSelectInput(session, "selected_projects",
                      choices  = master_choices,
                      selected = "__ALL__")
  })

  # --- Project filter buttons -------------------------------------------------
  observeEvent(input$sel_all, {
    updateSelectInput(session, "selected_projects", selected = "__ALL__")
  })
  observeEvent(input$sel_none, {
    updateSelectInput(session, "selected_projects", selected = "__NONE__")
  })
  observeEvent(input$sel_invert, {
    current <- input$selected_projects
    # Handle sentinel states first â€” invert of ALL is NONE and vice versa
    if (identical(current, "__ALL__")) {
      updateSelectInput(session, "selected_projects", selected = "__NONE__")
      return()
    }
    if (identical(current, "__NONE__")) {
      updateSelectInput(session, "selected_projects", selected = "__ALL__")
      return()
    }
    # Real project list: invert selection relative to all_projects
    inverted <- setdiff(all_projects, current)
    new_sel <- if (length(inverted) == 0) "__NONE__"
               else if (length(inverted) == length(all_projects)) "__ALL__"
               else inverted
    updateSelectInput(session, "selected_projects", selected = new_sel)
  })

  # resolve_sel: translate selectInput sentinels to a project vector.
  #   NULL or "__ALL__" -> return NULL  (All projects: callers skip the IN filter,
  #                                       so live projects absent from master are included)
  #   "__NONE__"        -> return character(0)  (None: callers filter to zero rows)
  #   character vector  -> return as-is  (specific selection)
  resolve_sel <- function(sel) {
    if (is.null(sel) || "__ALL__" %in% sel) return(NULL)
    if ("__NONE__" %in% sel) return(character(0))
    sel
  }

  # --- Overview ---------------------------------------------------------------
  output$vb_cost <- renderText({
    cc <- if (nrow(cc_d()) > 0) sum(cc_d()$totalCost, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
    gm <- if (nrow(gem_d()) > 0) sum(gem_d()$total_cost, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
    cx <- if (nrow(codex_d()) > 0 && "est_cost_usd" %in% names(codex_d()))
            sum(codex_d()$est_cost_usd, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
    sprintf("$%s", format(round(cc + gm + cx), big.mark = ","))
  })
  output$vb_tokens <- renderText({
    cc <- if (nrow(cc_d()) > 0) sum(cc_d()$totalTokens, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
    gm <- if (nrow(gem_d()) > 0) sum(gem_d()$total_tokens, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
    cx <- if (nrow(codex_d()) > 0 && "total_tokens" %in% names(codex_d()))
            sum(codex_d()$total_tokens, na.rm = TRUE) else 0
    total <- cc + gm + cx
    if (total >= 1e9) sprintf("%.1fB", total / 1e9)
    else if (total >= 1e6) sprintf("%.1fM", total / 1e6)
    else format(total, big.mark = ",")
  })
  output$vb_days <- renderText({
    dates <- c(
      if (nrow(cc_d()) > 0) unique(cc_d()$date),
      if (nrow(gem_d()) > 0) unique(gem_d()$date),
      if (nrow(codex_d()) > 0) unique(codex_d()$date)
    )
    as.character(length(unique(dates)))
  })
  output$vb_sessions <- renderText({
    n <- 0L
    if (has_rows(u_sessions)) {
      n <- nrow(u_sessions[u_sessions$date >= cutoff(), ])
    }
    if (has_rows(gem_sess)) {
      n <- n + nrow(gem_sess[gem_sess$date >= cutoff(), ])
    }
    # Include codex sessions (per-thread, not per-day-row) with the same cutoff
    if (has_rows(codex_sess) && "date" %in% names(codex_sess)) {
      n <- n + nrow(codex_sess[codex_sess$date >= cutoff(), ])
    }
    as.character(n)
  })

  output$overview_table <- renderDT({
    rows <- list()
    if (nrow(cc_d()) > 0) {
      d <- cc_d()
      rows[[1]] <- data.frame(
        Source = "Claude (ccusage)",
        Cost = sprintf("$%.2f", sum(d$totalCost, na.rm = TRUE)),
        `$/Day` = sprintf("$%.2f", sum(d$totalCost, na.rm = TRUE) / max(length(unique(d$date)), 1)),
        Tokens = format(sum(d$totalTokens, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ","),
        Days = length(unique(d$date)),
        Sessions = if (has_rows(u_sessions)) nrow(u_sessions[u_sessions$date >= cutoff(), ]) else 0L,
        `Date Range` = paste(min(d$date), "to", max(d$date)),
        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
    if (nrow(gem_d()) > 0) {
      d <- gem_d()
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Source = "Gemini",
        Cost = sprintf("$%.2f", sum(d$total_cost, na.rm = TRUE)),
        `$/Day` = sprintf("$%.2f", sum(d$total_cost, na.rm = TRUE) / max(length(unique(d$date)), 1)),
        Tokens = format(sum(d$total_tokens, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ","),
        Days = length(unique(d$date)),
        Sessions = if (has_rows(gem_sess)) nrow(gem_sess[gem_sess$date >= cutoff(), ]) else 0L,
        `Date Range` = paste(min(d$date), "to", max(d$date)),
        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
    if (nrow(codex_d()) > 0 && "est_cost_usd" %in% names(codex_d())) {
      d <- codex_d()
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Source = "Codex (est.)",
        Cost = sprintf("$%.2f", sum(d$est_cost_usd, na.rm = TRUE)),
        `$/Day` = sprintf("$%.2f", sum(d$est_cost_usd, na.rm = TRUE) / max(length(unique(d$date)), 1)),
        Tokens = format(sum(d$total_tokens, na.rm = TRUE), big.mark = ","),
        Days = length(unique(d$date)),
        Sessions = if (has_rows(codex_sess) && "date" %in% names(codex_sess))
                     nrow(codex_sess[codex_sess$date >= cutoff(), ]) else 0L,
        `Date Range` = paste(min(d$date), "to", max(d$date)),
        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
    if (!is.null(cmon) && !is.null(cmon$total_cost)) {
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        Source = "cmonitor",
        Cost = sprintf("$%.2f", cmon$total_cost),
        `$/Day` = "-",
        Tokens = format(cmon$total_tokens, big.mark = ","),
        Days = NA_integer_,
        Sessions = NA_integer_,
        `Date Range` = if (!is.null(cmon$date_range)) cmon$date_range else "-",
        check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
    tbl <- if (length(rows) > 0) do.call(rbind, rows) else data.frame()
    datatable(tbl, options = list(dom = "t", paging = FALSE, searching = FALSE,
        scrollX = TRUE, autoWidth = FALSE), rownames = FALSE)
  })

  output$overview_cost_ts <- renderUI({
    has_cc  <- nrow(cc_d()) > 0
    has_gem <- nrow(gem_d()) > 0
    has_cx  <- nrow(codex_d()) > 0 && "est_cost_usd" %in% names(codex_d())
    if (!has_cc && !has_gem && !has_cx) return(ec_empty(h = "350px"))

    # Aggregate each source by date
    cc_agg <- gem_agg <- cx_agg <- NULL
    if (has_cc) {
      cc_agg <- aggregate(totalCost ~ date, data = cc_d(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(cc_agg)[2] <- "claude"
    }
    if (has_gem) {
      gem_agg <- aggregate(total_cost ~ date, data = gem_d(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(gem_agg)[2] <- "gemini"
    }
    if (has_cx) {
      cx_agg <- aggregate(est_cost_usd ~ date, data = codex_d(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(cx_agg)[2] <- "codex"
    }

    # Merge all sources on date (full outer join chain)
    d <- data.frame(date = sort(unique(c(
      if (!is.null(cc_agg)) cc_agg$date,
      if (!is.null(gem_agg)) gem_agg$date,
      if (!is.null(cx_agg)) cx_agg$date
    ))))
    if (!is.null(cc_agg))  d <- merge(d, cc_agg,  by = "date", all.x = TRUE)
    if (!is.null(gem_agg)) d <- merge(d, gem_agg, by = "date", all.x = TRUE)
    if (!is.null(cx_agg))  d <- merge(d, cx_agg,  by = "date", all.x = TRUE)
    for (col in c("claude", "gemini", "codex")) {
      if (col %in% names(d)) d[[col]][is.na(d[[col]])] <- 0
    }
    d <- d[order(d$date), ]

    series <- list()
    if ("claude" %in% names(d))
      series[[length(series) + 1]] <- list(name = "Claude", type = "line",
        data = I(round(d$claude, 2)), itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude))
    if ("gemini" %in% names(d))
      series[[length(series) + 1]] <- list(name = "Gemini", type = "line",
        data = I(round(d$gemini, 2)), itemStyle = list(color = pal$gemini))
    if ("codex" %in% names(d))
      series[[length(series) + 1]] <- list(name = "Codex", type = "line",
        data = I(round(d$codex, 2)), itemStyle = list(color = pal$codex))
    ec_line(d$date, series, "350px", y_name = "Cost ($)")
  })

  # --- Cost Trends ------------------------------------------------------------
  output$cost_by_project <- renderUI({
    d <- cost_proj
    if (!is.data.frame(d) || nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No project cost estimates available"))
    # Apply time filter
    if (!is.null(cutoff())) d <- d[d$date >= cutoff(), ]
    # Apply project filter from canonical master list
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    if (!is.null(sel)) {
      d <- d[d$project %in% sel, ]
    }
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No data for selected filters"))

    all_d <- sort(unique(d$date))
    projects <- unique(d$project)
    series <- lapply(projects, function(p) {
      pd <- d[d$project == p, ]
      vals <- rep(0, length(all_d))
      vals[match(pd$date, all_d)] <- pd$est_cost
      list(name = p, type = "line", data = I(round(vals, 2)))
    })
    ec_line(all_d, series, y_name = "Estimated Cost ($)",
            aria_label = "Estimated daily cost by project (session-weighted)")
  })

  output$cost_cumulative <- renderUI({
    d <- cost_proj
    if (!is.data.frame(d) || nrow(d) == 0)
      return(ec_empty("No project cost estimates available"))
    if (!is.null(cutoff())) d <- d[d$date >= cutoff(), ]
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    if (!is.null(sel)) d <- d[d$project %in% sel, ]
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No data for selected projects / period"))

    daily <- aggregate(est_cost ~ date, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    daily <- daily[order(daily$date), ]
    series <- list(list(
      name = "Total (filtered)", type = "line",
      data = I(round(cumsum(daily$est_cost), 2)),
      itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude),
      areaStyle = list(opacity = 0.1)
    ))
    ec_line(daily$date, series, y_name = "Cumulative Cost ($)",
            aria_label = "Cumulative cost for selected projects")
  })

  output$cost_weekly <- renderUI({
    # W5 from #72: Weekly Cost by Source â€” Claude, Gemini, Codex
    # SOURCE_PALETTE is used for all three series (narrative-colour-persistence rule)
    rows <- list()
    if (nrow(cc_d()) > 0) {
      d <- cc_d()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(totalCost ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      a$source <- "Claude"
      rows[[1]] <- a
    }
    if (nrow(gem_d()) > 0) {
      d <- gem_d()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(total_cost ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "totalCost"
      a$source <- "Gemini"
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- a
    }
    if (nrow(codex_d_proj()) > 0 && "est_cost_usd" %in% names(codex_d_proj())) {
      d <- codex_d_proj()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(est_cost_usd ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "totalCost"
      a$source <- "Codex"
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- a
    }
    if (length(rows) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    d <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    all_weeks <- sort(unique(d$week))
    sources <- unique(d$source)
    src_color <- c(Claude = SOURCE_PALETTE[["claude"]],
                   Gemini = SOURCE_PALETTE[["gemini"]],
                   Codex  = SOURCE_PALETTE[["codex"]])
    series <- lapply(sources, function(s) {
      sd <- d[d$source == s, ]
      vals <- rep(0, length(all_weeks))
      vals[match(sd$week, all_weeks)] <- sd$totalCost
      list(name = s, type = "scatter", symbol = "circle", symbolSize = 10,
           data = I(round(vals, 2)),
           itemStyle = list(color = unname(src_color[s])))
    })
    ec_bar(all_weeks, series)
  })

  output$cost_by_model <- renderUI({
    d <- cc_d()
    if (nrow(d) == 0 || is.null(d$modelsUsed)) return(ec_empty())
    # Expand rows by model
    models_split <- strsplit(as.character(d$modelsUsed), ",\\s*")
    n_each <- lengths(models_split)
    d_exp <- d[rep(seq_len(nrow(d)), n_each), ]
    d_exp$model <- clean_model(unlist(models_split))
    d_exp <- d_exp[d_exp$model != "", ]
    d_exp$week <- floor_date(d_exp$date, "week")
    agg <- aggregate(totalCost ~ week + model, data = d_exp, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    all_weeks <- sort(unique(agg$week))
    models <- unique(agg$model)
    series <- lapply(models, function(m) {
      md <- agg[agg$model == m, ]
      vals <- rep(0, length(all_weeks))
      vals[match(md$week, all_weeks)] <- md$totalCost
      list(name = m, type = "line", data = I(round(vals, 2)))
    })
    ec_line(all_weeks, series)
  })

  # --- Token Analysis ---------------------------------------------------------
  output$token_daily <- renderUI({
    has_cc <- nrow(cc_d_proj()) > 0
    has_gem <- nrow(gem_d()) > 0
    if (!has_cc && !has_gem) return(ec_empty(h = "350px"))

    cc_tok <- gem_tok <- NULL
    if (has_cc) {
      cc_tok <- aggregate(totalTokens ~ date, data = cc_d_proj(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(cc_tok)[2] <- "claude"
    }
    if (has_gem) {
      gem_tok <- aggregate(total_tokens ~ date, data = gem_d(), FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(gem_tok)[2] <- "gemini"
    }

    if (has_cc && has_gem) {
      d <- merge(cc_tok, gem_tok, by = "date", all = TRUE)
      d$claude[is.na(d$claude)] <- 0
      d$gemini[is.na(d$gemini)] <- 0
      d <- d[order(d$date), ]
      series <- list(
        list(name = "Claude", type = "line", data = I(d$claude),
             itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude)),
        list(name = "Gemini", type = "line", data = I(d$gemini),
             itemStyle = list(color = pal$gemini)))
      ec_line(d$date, series, "350px", y_name = "Tokens (M)")
    } else if (has_cc) {
      cc_tok <- cc_tok[order(cc_tok$date), ]
      ec_line(cc_tok$date, list(list(name = "Claude", type = "line",
        data = I(cc_tok$claude), itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude))), "350px",
        y_name = "Tokens (M)")
    } else {
      gem_tok <- gem_tok[order(gem_tok$date), ]
      ec_line(gem_tok$date, list(list(name = "Gemini", type = "line",
        data = I(gem_tok$gemini), itemStyle = list(color = pal$gemini))), "350px",
        y_name = "Tokens (M)")
    }
  })

  output$token_comp <- renderUI({
    d <- cc_d_proj()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    all_d <- sort(unique(d$date))
    val_at <- function(agg, col, dates) {
      vals <- rep(0, length(dates))
      vals[match(agg$date, dates)] <- agg[[col]]
      vals
    }
    series <- list()
    # Use whichever column names exist (cmonitor vs raw ccusage format)
    tok_cols <- list(
      Input = intersect(c("inputTokens"), names(d)),
      Output = intersect(c("outputTokens"), names(d)),
      `Cache Create` = intersect(c("cacheCreation", "cacheCreationTokens"), names(d)),
      `Cache Read` = intersect(c("cacheRead", "cacheReadTokens"), names(d))
    )
    for (nm in names(tok_cols)) {
      col <- tok_cols[[nm]]
      if (length(col) > 0 && col[1] %in% names(d)) {
        agg <- aggregate(d[[col[1]]] ~ date, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
        names(agg)[2] <- "val"
        series[[length(series) + 1]] <- list(
          name = nm, type = "bar", stack = "tokens",
          data = I(val_at(agg, "val", all_d))
        )
      }
    }
    if (length(series) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    ec_bar(all_d, series)
  })

  output$token_weekly <- renderUI({
    # W6 from #72: Weekly Tokens by Source â€” Claude, Gemini, Codex
    rows <- list()
    if (nrow(cc_d_proj()) > 0) {
      d <- cc_d_proj()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(totalTokens ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      a$source <- "Claude"
      rows[[1]] <- a
    }
    if (nrow(gem_d()) > 0) {
      d <- gem_d()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(total_tokens ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "totalTokens"
      a$source <- "Gemini"
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- a
    }
    if (nrow(codex_d_proj()) > 0 && "total_tokens" %in% names(codex_d_proj())) {
      d <- codex_d_proj()
      d$week <- floor_date(d$date, "week")
      a <- aggregate(total_tokens ~ week, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
      names(a)[2] <- "totalTokens"
      a$source <- "Codex"
      rows[[length(rows) + 1]] <- a
    }
    if (length(rows) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    d <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    all_weeks <- sort(unique(d$week))
    sources <- unique(d$source)
    src_color <- c(Claude = SOURCE_PALETTE[["claude"]],
                   Gemini = SOURCE_PALETTE[["gemini"]],
                   Codex  = SOURCE_PALETTE[["codex"]])
    series <- lapply(sources, function(s) {
      sd <- d[d$source == s, ]
      vals <- rep(0, length(all_weeks))
      vals[match(sd$week, all_weeks)] <- sd$totalTokens
      list(name = s, type = "scatter", symbol = "circle", symbolSize = 10,
           data = I(vals),
           itemStyle = list(color = unname(src_color[s])))
    })
    ec_bar(all_weeks, series)
  })

  # --- Sessions ---------------------------------------------------------------
  # Summary value boxes
  # Helper: filter u_sessions by time cutoff + selected_projects
  sess_filtered <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(u_sessions)) return(data.frame())
    d <- u_sessions[u_sessions$date >= cutoff(), ]
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    if (!is.null(sel) && "project_short" %in% names(d))
      d <- d[d$project_short %in% sel, ]
    d
  })

  output$vb_sess_count <- renderText({
    as.character(nrow(sess_filtered()))
  })
  output$vb_sess_duration <- renderText({
    d <- sess_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return("0 min")
    total <- sum(d$duration_min, na.rm = TRUE)
    if (total >= 60) sprintf("%.1f hr", total / 60) else sprintf("%.0f min", total)
  })
  output$vb_sess_avg <- renderText({
    d <- sess_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return("0 min")
    avg <- mean(d$duration_min, na.rm = TRUE)
    sprintf("%.1f min", avg)
  })

  output$sess_claude <- renderDT({
    d <- sess_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(datatable(data.frame(Message = "No sessions for selected projects")))
    d <- d[order(d$started_at, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    d <- head(d, input$top_n)
    out <- data.frame(
      Project = if ("project_short" %in% names(d)) d$project_short else d$project,
      Started = paste(d$date, d$time_str),  # Use pre-extracted time
      `Duration (min)` = ifelse(is.na(d$duration_min), "â€”", sprintf("%.0f", d$duration_min)),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
      check.names = FALSE
    )
    datatable(out, options = list(dom = "t", pageLength = 20, scrollX = TRUE), rownames = FALSE)
  })

  output$sess_gemini <- renderDT({
    if (!has_rows(gem_sess)) return(datatable(data.frame()))
    d <- gem_sess[!is.na(gem_sess$date) & gem_sess$date >= cutoff(), ]
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(datatable(data.frame(Message = "No Gemini sessions in selected time horizon")))
    d <- d[order(d$total_cost, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    d <- head(d, input$top_n)
    out <- data.frame(
      Session = d$sessionId,
      Project = d$project,
      Cost = sprintf("$%.2f", d$total_cost),
      Tokens = format(d$total_tokens, big.mark = ","),
      Started = d$start_time,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    datatable(out, options = list(dom = "t", pageLength = 20, scrollX = TRUE), rownames = FALSE)
  })

  output$sess_hist <- renderUI({
    d <- sess_filtered()
    d <- d[!is.na(d$duration_min) & d$duration_min > 0, ]
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No sessions for selected projects"))
    h <- hist(d$duration_min, breaks = 20, plot = FALSE)
    ec_bar(round(h$mids, 1), list(
      list(name = "Sessions", type = "bar", data = I(h$counts),
           itemStyle = list(color = pal$hist))))
  })

  # sess_scatter migrated to DuckDB-WASM (#83 Phase 2): observe sends "duckdb_sessions_time"
  # message to JS queryAndRenderSessionsTime(), which queries sessions.parquet directly.
  observe({
    req(input$tabs == "Usage", input$usage_tabs == "Sessions")
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    projects_json <- if (is.null(sel)) list()
                     else if (length(sel) == 0L) "__NONE__"
                     else as.list(sel)
    cutoff_str <- if (!is.null(cutoff())) format(cutoff(), "%Y-%m-%d") else NULL
    session$sendCustomMessage("duckdb_sessions_time", list(
      projects = projects_json,
      min_date = cutoff_str
    ))
  })

  # --- By Project -------------------------------------------------------------
  # Session-weighted estimated cost by project
  proj_filtered <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(cost_proj)) return(data.frame())
    d <- cost_proj
    d$date <- as.Date(d$date)
    d <- d[d$date >= cutoff(), ]
    # project column already normalised to canonical_project at load time
    d$project_short <- d$project
    # Apply project filter from master list
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    if (!is.null(sel))
      d <- d[d$project_short %in% sel, ]
    d
  })

  output$proj_cost_total <- renderUI({
    d <- proj_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    # Aggregate total cost by project
    agg <- aggregate(est_cost ~ project_short, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    agg <- agg[order(agg$est_cost, decreasing = FALSE), ]  # Ascending for dot chart
    # Cleveland dot chart (horizontal)
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "axis", axisPointer = list(type = "shadow")),
      grid = list(left = 120, containLabel = FALSE),
      xAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Est. Cost ($)"),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(agg$project_short)),
      series = list(list(
        type = "scatter",
        data = I(round(agg$est_cost, 2)),
        symbolSize = 12,
        itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude)
      ))
    ), h = "300px", aria_label = "Cleveland dot chart: estimated cost by project")
  })

  observe({
    req(input$tabs == "Usage", input$usage_tabs == "By Project")
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    # NULL        => All projects: send null  (JS omits IN filter, includes live projects)
    # character(0)=> None selected: send "__NONE__" sentinel (JS renders empty chart)
    # character[] => specific selection: send as JSON array
    projects_json <- if (is.null(sel)) list()
                     else if (length(sel) == 0L) "__NONE__"
                     else as.list(sel)
    cutoff_str <- if (!is.null(cutoff())) format(cutoff(), "%Y-%m-%d") else NULL
    session$sendCustomMessage("duckdb_sessions", list(
      projects = projects_json,
      min_date = cutoff_str
    ))
    # Phase 5 (#83): Session Duration by Project migrated to DuckDB-WASM
    session$sendCustomMessage("duckdb_sessions_duration", list(
      projects = projects_json,
      min_date = cutoff_str
    ))
  })

  output$proj_cost_daily <- renderUI({
    d <- proj_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    # Get top N projects by total cost
    totals <- aggregate(est_cost ~ project_short, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    top_proj <- head(totals[order(totals$est_cost, decreasing = TRUE), "project_short"], 8)
    d <- d[d$project_short %in% top_proj, ]
    # Pivot to wide format for stacked area
    all_dates <- sort(unique(d$date))
    series <- lapply(top_proj, function(p) {
      p_data <- d[d$project_short == p, c("date", "est_cost")]
      # Merge with all dates to fill gaps
      merged <- merge(data.frame(date = all_dates), p_data, by = "date", all.x = TRUE)
      merged$est_cost[is.na(merged$est_cost)] <- 0
      list(
        name = p, type = "line", stack = "total", areaStyle = list(),
        data = I(round(merged$est_cost[order(merged$date)], 2))
      )
    })
    ec_line(all_dates, series, h = "350px", y_name = "Cost ($)",
            aria_label = "Stacked area chart: daily estimated cost by project")
  })

  output$proj_cost_cumulative <- renderUI({
    d <- proj_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    totals <- aggregate(est_cost ~ project_short, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    top_proj <- head(totals[order(totals$est_cost, decreasing = TRUE), "project_short"], 8)
    d <- d[d$project_short %in% top_proj, ]
    all_dates <- sort(unique(d$date))
    series <- lapply(top_proj, function(p) {
      p_data <- d[d$project_short == p, c("date", "est_cost")]
      merged <- merge(data.frame(date = all_dates), p_data, by = "date", all.x = TRUE)
      merged$est_cost[is.na(merged$est_cost)] <- 0
      merged <- merged[order(merged$date), ]
      list(name = p, type = "line", data = I(round(cumsum(merged$est_cost), 2)))
    })
    ec_line(all_dates, series, h = "350px", y_name = "Cumulative Cost ($)",
            aria_label = "Cumulative estimated cost by project")
  })

  # proj_duration migrated to DuckDB-WASM (#83 Phase 5): observer sends "duckdb_sessions_duration"
  # message to JS queryAndRenderDuration(), which queries sessions.parquet directly.
  # SQL: SELECT canonical_project, ROUND(SUM(duration_min), 1) AS total_min
  #      FROM sessions.parquet WHERE ... GROUP BY canonical_project ORDER BY total_min DESC

  output$proj_share_heatmap <- renderUI({
    d <- proj_filtered()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    # Get top 10 projects by total cost
    totals <- aggregate(est_cost ~ project_short, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    top_proj <- head(totals[order(totals$est_cost, decreasing = TRUE), "project_short"], 10)
    d <- d[d$project_short %in% top_proj, ]
    # Convert to heatmap data: [x_index, y_index, value]
    dates <- sort(unique(d$date))
    projs <- top_proj
    heat_data <- list()
    for (i in seq_along(dates)) {
      for (j in seq_along(projs)) {
        val <- d[d$date == dates[i] & d$project_short == projs[j], "share"]
        val <- if (length(val) == 0) 0 else round(val[1] * 100, 1)
        heat_data[[length(heat_data) + 1]] <- list(i - 1, j - 1, val)
      }
    }
    # Build spec without JS() wrappers to avoid JS_EVAL serialization error.
    # The tooltip formatter is injected as a raw JS literal after toJSON().
    spec <- list(
      tooltip = list(position = "top", formatter = "__SHARE_FMT__"),
      grid = list(left = 100, bottom = 60, containLabel = FALSE),
      xAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(as.character(dates)), splitArea = list(show = TRUE)),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(projs), splitArea = list(show = TRUE)),
      visualMap = list(min = 0, max = 100, calculable = TRUE,
                       orient = "horizontal", left = "center", bottom = 5,
                       inRange = list(color = c("#0d1117", "#238636", "#40c463"))),
      series = list(list(
        name = "Share", type = "heatmap", data = heat_data,
        emphasis = list(itemStyle = list(shadowBlur = 10, shadowColor = "rgba(0,0,0,0.5)"))
      ))
    )
    id <- paste0("ec", sample.int(1e7, 1))
    j <- toJSON(spec, auto_unbox = TRUE, null = "null")
    j <- gsub('"__SHARE_FMT__"', 'function(p){return p.value[2]+"% share"}', j, fixed = TRUE)
    tagList(
      tags$div(id = id, class = "echart-div", role = "button",
               `aria-label` = "Heatmap: project cost share percentage by date",
               tabindex = "0", style = "width:100%;height:300px;cursor:zoom-in"),
      tags$script(HTML(paste0(
        "(function(){var c=echarts.init(document.getElementById('", id, "'),'dark');",
        "c.setOption(", j, ");new ResizeObserver(function(){c.resize()})",
        ".observe(document.getElementById('", id, "'));})();"
      )))
    )
  })

  # --- Phase 2: Weekly Commit Heatmap (By Project tab) -----------------------
  output$proj_weekly_heatmap <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(weekly_commits)) return(ec_empty("No weekly commit data available"))
    d <- weekly_commits
    # Filter to selected repos if sidebar has a selection
    if (!is.null(input$commit_project) && input$commit_project != "all" &&
        "project" %in% names(d)) {
      d <- d[d$project == input$commit_project, ]
    }
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No data for selected repo"))
    ec_heatmap(d, x = "iso_week", y = "project", value = "n_commits",
               h = "350px",
               aria_label = "Weekly commit heatmap: commit counts by week and project")
  })

  # --- Phase 5 (#83): Cost-per-Commit Trend migrated to DuckDB-WASM ----------
  # output$cost_per_commit_chart renderUI removed â€” replaced by queryAndRenderCostPerCommit()
  # JS function that queries costs.parquet JOIN git_commits.parquet client-side.
  # Fixed div#cost_per_commit_ec is in the UI. Observer below triggers re-query
  # when the Cost Trends tab is active and filter/granularity inputs change.
  observe({
    req(input$tabs == "Usage", input$usage_tabs == "Cost Trends")
    sel <- resolve_sel(input$selected_projects)
    projects_json <- if (is.null(sel)) list()
                     else if (length(sel) == 0L) "__NONE__"
                     else as.list(sel)
    cutoff_str <- if (!is.null(cutoff())) format(cutoff(), "%Y-%m-%d") else NULL
    gran <- if (!is.null(input$granularity)) input$granularity else "weekly"
    session$sendCustomMessage("duckdb_cost_per_commit", list(
      projects    = projects_json,
      min_date    = cutoff_str,
      granularity = gran
    ))
  })

  # --- Phase 2: File Churn DataTable (Git tab) --------------------------------
  output$file_churn_table <- renderDT({
    if (!has_rows(file_churn_data)) return(datatable(data.frame(Message = "No file churn data")))
    d <- file_churn_data
    # Filter to selected repo if applicable
    if (!is.null(input$commit_project) && input$commit_project != "all" &&
        "project" %in% names(d)) {
      d <- d[d$project == input$commit_project, ]
    }
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(datatable(data.frame(Message = "No data for selected repo")))
    # Select display columns
    cols <- intersect(c("project", "file", "n_commits", "total_lines_changed", "last_changed_date"),
                      names(d))
    out <- d[order(d$total_lines_changed, decreasing = TRUE), cols, drop = FALSE]
    # Shorten file path for display
    out$file <- basename(out$file)
    datatable(out,
      caption = "File churn by lines changed (top files per project)",
      options = list(
        pageLength = 15, dom = "ftip", scrollX = TRUE, scrollY = "350px",
        order = list(list(which(cols == "total_lines_changed") - 1L, "desc"))
      ),
      rownames = FALSE
    )
  })

  # --- Phase 2: Change Coupling Force Graph (Git tab) -------------------------
  output$change_coupling_graph <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(change_coupling_data)) return(ec_empty("No coupling data available"))
    d <- change_coupling_data
    # Filter to selected repo if applicable
    if (!is.null(input$commit_project) && input$commit_project != "all" &&
        "project" %in% names(d)) {
      d <- d[d$project == input$commit_project, ]
    }
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty("No coupling data for selected repo"))
    # Cap to top 60 pairs to keep graph readable
    d <- head(d[order(d$weight_normalised, decreasing = TRUE), ], 60)

    # Build unique nodes
    all_files <- unique(c(d$file_a, d$file_b))
    proj_lookup <- character(length(all_files))
    names(proj_lookup) <- all_files
    for (f in all_files) {
      rows_a <- d[d$file_a == f, "project"]
      rows_b <- d[d$file_b == f, "project"]
      proj_lookup[f] <- c(rows_a, rows_b)[1]
    }
    cat_map <- c("llm" = 0L, "llmtelemetry" = 1L)
    # (#888) Use full repo-relative path as node id to avoid collisions when
    # two files share the same basename (e.g. R/utils.R and tests/utils.R).
    # basename() is kept only as the human-readable display label.
    # ECharts graph series matches edge source/target to node id when id is
    # provided, so edges also use full paths as identifiers.
    nodes <- lapply(all_files, function(f) {
      cat_idx <- if (!is.na(proj_lookup[f]) && proj_lookup[f] %in% names(cat_map)) {
        cat_map[[proj_lookup[f]]]
      } else {
        2L
      }
      list(name = f, value = 1,
           symbolSize = 10,
           category = cat_idx,
           label = list(formatter = basename(f)))
    })

    edges <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(d)), function(i) {
      w <- d$weight_normalised[i]
      list(source = d$file_a[i],
           target = d$file_b[i],
           n_cochanges = d$n_cochanges[i],
           lineStyle = list(width = max(1, round(w * 5)),
                            opacity = max(0.3, w)))
    })

    ec_force_graph(nodes, edges, h = "450px",
      aria_label = "Force-directed graph: files that change together. Node = file, edge = co-change count.")
  })

  # --- Time Blocks ------------------------------------------------------------
  blk <- reactive({
    if (!has_rows(cc_blocks)) return(data.frame())
    cc_blocks[cc_blocks$date >= (date_max - input$block_days), ]
  })

  output$block_table <- renderDT({
    d <- blk()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(datatable(data.frame()))
    # Sort most-recent-first so display order is newest at top
    d <- d[order(d$startTime, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    n <- nrow(d)
    eff_end <- coalesce2(d$actualEndTime, d$endTime)
    out <- data.frame(
      Day = format(d$date, "%a %Y-%m-%d"),
      Start = format(d$startTime, "%H:%M"),
      End = format(eff_end, "%H:%M"),
      Duration = sprintf("%.1fh", d$duration_hrs),
      Cost = sprintf("$%.2f", d$costUSD),
      `$/hr` = sprintf("$%.2f", d$cost_per_hr),
      Tokens = fmt_tokens(if ("totalTokens" %in% names(d)) d$totalTokens else rep(0, n)),
      `Tok/hr` = fmt_tokens(if ("tok_per_hr" %in% names(d)) d$tok_per_hr else rep(0, n)),
      Models = if ("models" %in% names(d)) clean_model(d$models) else rep("-", n),
      check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    datatable(out,
      extensions = "RowGroup",
      options = list(
        pageLength = 50, dom = "rtip", scrollX = TRUE,
        rowGroup = list(
          dataSrc = 0,
          startRender = DT::JS("function(rows, group) {
            var dur=0, cost=0, tok=0;
            function pTok(s){s=s.trim();if(s.endsWith('B'))return parseFloat(s)*1e9;if(s.endsWith('M'))return parseFloat(s)*1e6;if(s.endsWith('k'))return parseFloat(s)*1e3;return parseInt(s.replace(/,/g,''))||0;}
            function fTok(n){if(n>=1e9)return(n/1e9).toFixed(1)+'B';if(n>=1e6)return(n/1e6).toFixed(1)+'M';if(n>=1e3)return Math.round(n/1e3)+'k';return n.toString();}
            rows.every(function() {
              var d=this.data();
              dur += parseFloat(d[3]) || 0;
              cost += parseFloat(d[4].replace('$','')) || 0;
              tok += pTok(d[6]);
            });
            return $('<tr style=\"font-weight:bold;background:#2a2a2a\"/>')
              .append('<td colspan=\"2\">' + group + '</td>')
              .append('<td>' + dur.toFixed(1) + 'h</td>')
              .append('<td>$' + cost.toFixed(2) + '</td>')
              .append('<td colspan=\"2\">' + fTok(tok) + ' tok</td>')
              .append('<td colspan=\"2\">' + rows.count() + ' blocks</td>');
          }")
        ),
        columnDefs = list(
          list(visible = FALSE, targets = 0),
          list(targets = 8, render = DT::JS(
            "function(data, type, row) { return '<span style=\"white-space:nowrap\">' + data + '</span>'; }"
          ))
        ),
        order = list()  # preserve server-side sort (d$startTime decreasing) â€” #896
      ),
      rownames = FALSE)
  })

  output$block_timeline <- renderUI({
    d <- blk()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    d <- d[order(d$startTime), ]
    pts <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(d)), function(i)
      list(round(d$costUSD[i], 2), round(d$duration_hrs[i], 2), round(d$costUSD[i], 2)))
    id <- paste0("ec", sample.int(1e7, 1))
    opt <- list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "item",
        formatter = "{c}"),
      grid = list(containLabel = TRUE),
      xAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Cost ($)"),
      yAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Duration (h)"),
      series = list(list(type = "scatter", data = pts,
                         itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude)))
    )
    j <- toJSON(opt, auto_unbox = TRUE, null = "null")
    tagList(
      tags$div(id = id, class = "echart-div", role = "button",
               `aria-label` = "Cost vs duration scatter chart â€” click to enlarge",
               tabindex = "0", style = "width:100%;height:300px;cursor:zoom-in"),
      tags$script(HTML(paste0(
        "(function(){var c=echarts.init(document.getElementById('", id, "'),'dark');",
        "var o=", j, ";",
        "o.series[0].symbolSize=function(d){return Math.max(3,Math.sqrt(d[2])*4)};",
        "o.tooltip.formatter=function(p){return '$'+p.value[0].toFixed(2)+' | '+p.value[1].toFixed(1)+'h'};",
        "c.setOption(o);new ResizeObserver(function(){c.resize()})",
        ".observe(document.getElementById('", id, "'));",
        "function fs(){var url=c.getDataURL({type:'png',pixelRatio:2,backgroundColor:'#000'});",
        "var ov=document.createElement('div');ov.className='fullscreen-overlay';",
        "var img=document.createElement('img');img.src=url;ov.appendChild(img);",
        "ov.onclick=function(){ov.remove()};document.body.appendChild(ov)}",
        "document.getElementById('", id, "').addEventListener('click',fs);",
        "document.getElementById('", id, "').addEventListener('keydown',function(e){",
        "if(e.key==='Enter'||e.key===' '){e.preventDefault();fs()}})})();"
      )))
    )
  })

  output$block_heatmap <- renderUI({
    d <- blk()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    d$wday_chr <- as.character(d$wday)
    heat_agg <- aggregate(costUSD ~ wday_chr + hour, data = d, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE)
    day_names <- c("Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat", "Sun")
    all_combos <- expand.grid(hour = 0:23, wday_chr = day_names, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    heat <- merge(all_combos, heat_agg, by = c("wday_chr", "hour"), all.x = TRUE)
    heat$costUSD[is.na(heat$costUSD)] <- 0
    heat_data <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(heat)), function(i)
      list(heat$hour[i], match(heat$wday_chr[i], day_names) - 1, round(heat$costUSD[i], 2)))
    max_cost <- max(heat$costUSD, na.rm = TRUE)
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "item"),
      grid = list(containLabel = TRUE),
      xAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(0:23), name = "Hour"),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(day_names)),
      visualMap = list(min = 0, max = max(max_cost, 1), calculable = TRUE,
        inRange = list(color = I(c("#1a1a1a", pal$orange, pal$gemini))),
        textStyle = list(color = "#ccc")),
      series = list(list(type = "heatmap", data = heat_data,
        label = list(show = FALSE)))
    ))
  })

  # --- Commits -----------------------------------------------------------------
  # Populate project filter from the multi-project data
  observe({
    if (!has_rows(git_commits_proj)) return()
    projs <- sort(unique(git_commits_proj$project))
    updateSelectInput(session, "commit_project",
      choices = c("All repos" = "all", setNames(projs, projs)),
      selected = "all")
  })

  commits_filt <- reactive({
    d <- if (has_rows(git_commits_proj)) git_commits_proj else git_commits
    if (!has_rows(d)) return(data.frame())
    d <- d[!is.na(d$date) & d$date >= cutoff(), ]
    proj_sel <- input$commit_project
    if (!is.null(proj_sel) && proj_sel != "all" && "project" %in% names(d))
      d <- d[d$project == proj_sel, ]
    d
  })

  output$commits_by_proj <- renderDT({
    d <- commits_filt()  # already filtered by cutoff() + commit_project (#815/#816)
    if (!has_rows(d)) return(DT::datatable(data.frame()))
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(DT::datatable(data.frame()))
    proj_col <- if ("project" %in% names(d)) d$project else rep("llmtelemetry", nrow(d))
    splits   <- split(d, proj_col)
    if (length(splits) == 0) return(DT::datatable(data.frame()))
    summary_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(splits), function(i) {
      g <- splits[[i]]
      if (is.null(g) || nrow(g) == 0) return(NULL)
      lc  <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(g$lines_changed))
      la  <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(g$lines_added))
      ld  <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(g$lines_deleted))
      med <- if (any(!is.na(lc))) round(median(lc, na.rm = TRUE)) else NA_real_
      mn  <- if (any(!is.na(lc))) round(mean(lc,   na.rm = TRUE)) else NA_real_
      data.frame(
        Project     = names(splits)[[i]],
        Commits     = nrow(g),
        Med_Lines   = med,
        Mean_Lines  = mn,
        Lines_Added = sum(la, na.rm = TRUE),
        Lines_Del   = sum(ld, na.rm = TRUE),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }))
    if (is.null(summary_df) || nrow(summary_df) == 0) return(DT::datatable(data.frame()))
    summary_df <- summary_df[order(summary_df$Commits, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    DT::datatable(summary_df,
      rownames = FALSE,
      caption = "Commit activity by project (selected time window)",
      options = list(pageLength = 10, dom = "t", order = list(list(1, "desc"))),
      class = "compact stripe"
    )
  })

  output$vb_commits <- renderText({
    d <- commits_filt()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return("0")
    as.character(nrow(d))
  })
  output$vb_median_lines <- renderText({
    d <- commits_filt()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return("0")
    as.character(round(median(d$lines_changed, na.rm = TRUE)))
  })
  output$vb_mean_lines <- renderText({
    d <- commits_filt()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return("0")
    as.character(round(mean(d$lines_changed, na.rm = TRUE)))
  })

  output$commit_hist <- renderUI({
    d <- commits_filt()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty(h = "350px"))
    h <- hist(d$lines_changed, breaks = 30, plot = FALSE)
    ec_bar(round(h$mids), list(
      list(name = "Commits", type = "bar", data = I(h$counts),
           itemStyle = list(color = pal$hist))), "350px")
  })

  output$commit_scatter_time <- renderUI({
    d <- commits_filt()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    pts <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(d)), function(i)
      list(as.character(d$date[i]), d$lines_changed[i], d$files_changed[i]))
    ec_scatter(pts, pal$claude, x_type = "time", size_col = 3, scale = 3)
  })

  output$commit_scatter_add_del <- renderUI({
    d <- commits_filt()
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(ec_empty())
    pts <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(d)), function(i)
      list(d$lines_added[i], d$lines_deleted[i], d$files_changed[i]))
    ec_scatter(pts, pal$gemini, size_col = 3, scale = 3)
  })

  # --- Repo Health ------------------------------------------------------------
  bus_dot <- function(d, label = "Bus factor dot chart") {
    if (!has_rows(d)) return(ec_empty())
    d <- head(d[order(d$commits, decreasing = TRUE), ], 10)
    d <- d[nrow(d):1, ]  # reverse for bottom-up display
    total <- sum(d$commits)
    labels <- paste0(d$author, " (", round(d$commits / total * 100), "%)")
    ec(list(
      tooltip = list(trigger = "axis"),
      toolbox = list(feature = list(
        dataZoom = list(), restore = list(), saveAsImage = list()
      )),
      dataZoom = list(list(type = "inside", xAxisIndex = 0)),
      grid = list(containLabel = TRUE, left = 120),
      xAxis = list(type = "value", name = "Commits"),
      yAxis = list(type = "category", data = I(labels)),
      series = list(list(type = "scatter", data = I(d$commits),
        symbol = "circle", symbolSize = 14,
        itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude),
        label = list(show = TRUE, position = "right", color = "#ccc",
                     distance = 8)))
    ), aria_label = label)
  }
  output$rh_bus_all <- renderUI(bus_dot(git_bus$all_time, "Bus factor all time: contributor commit share"))
  output$rh_bus_6mo <- renderUI(bus_dot(git_bus$recent_6mo, "Bus factor 6 months: recent contributor concentration"))

  output$rh_velocity <- renderUI({
    if (!has_rows(git_vel)) return(ec_empty())
    d <- git_vel[order(git_vel$month), ]
    ec_line(d$month, list(
      list(name = "Commits", type = "line", data = I(d$commits),
           itemStyle = list(color = pal$claude),
           areaStyle = list(opacity = 0.3))),
